mechanism_narrative: Increases in the federal funds rate directly raise the discount rate for equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0 percent threshold, labor market deterioration signals an imminent recession, causing equity investors to price in negative earnings revisions over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real personal income and housing starts indicates a synchronized collapse in consumer demand and construction activity, driving negative S&P 500 returns as corporate revenue forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns when SPX volatility remains below 15 percent.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hike concerns, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles yield negative 20-day forward returns in greater than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth coincides with negative 60-day forward returns in greater than 55 percent of quarters over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond 3.5 percent, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, triggering negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 3.5 percent year-over-year are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 65 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS and UNRATE divergence signal yields negative returns when credit spreads widen by more than 50 basis points concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below 1200 thousand units signals a severe downturn in residential investment, dragging down related sector earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 1200 thousand units are followed by positive 60-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observations.
